name: Unit Testing
def: Testing the smallest isolated units of code (functions/classes) in isolation.
problem: Catch defects early and lock in behaviour of individual components for safe refactoring.
mechanism: Arrange-act-assert: set up inputs, call the unit, assert outputs/effects; dependencies are stubbed/mocked to isolate the unit.
limits: Over-mocking tests the mocks, not reality
whenNot: Glue code dominated by external interactions (favour integration tests)
myths: "100% unit coverage = bug-free" — coverage ≠ correctness; mutation testing disproves it
